rationale: Built the branch binary (62cd6df3, go build -o /tmp/mindspec-fbel2-s3 ./cmd/mindspec) and ran `validate spec` against 085-094 + 110 (the exact corpus my lens names): 086/087/088/089/091/092/093/094/110 pass clean; 085 and 090 fail with `impacted-domains-resolve` (SevError) on a path-like Impacted-Domains entry containing markdown-table artifact characters (backtick/bold-marker). I then ran `validate plan` on those same two specs against the UNMODIFIED plan.go and got the byte-identical error code, message, and SevError severity -- confirming R5 is true parity (spec-approve now catches what plan-approve already rejected), not new strictness, exactly as the diff claims. R6 was exercised on 7/11 of these real specs (each has >=1 anchored ADR Touchpoints link) with zero false positives. plan.go/plan_test.go/internal/approve have ZERO diff anywhere in this branch vs main (git diff main...HEAD -- those paths is empty), so plan-approve behavior is trivially byte-identical; go test -count=1 ./internal/validate ./internal/approve both pass fresh (192s/19s, no cache). Doc-sync: `validate docs --diff HEAD~1` on this commit emits only the pre-existing missing-source-globs warning, no missing-doc-sync error -- the architecture.md addition lands in the same commit as the code it documents, and I checked its prose line-by-line against the diff (function names, error codes, regex, R7c boundary) with no drift. I independently reproduced the 085/090 flip that F1/G1 also found; unlike G1's REJECT framing (branch vs installed main binary), the correct comparison is branch-spec-approve vs unmodified-branch-plan-approve, which I confirm match exactly -- and no test or CI workflow in the repo re-validates the historical spec corpus, so this doesn't break anything self-consistent today.
findings: 085-executor-boundary and 090-production-hygiene (both merged, approved specs) newly fail `validate spec` with `impacted-domains-resolve` (SevError) due to R5 -- but running `validate plan` on the same two specs against the completely unmodified plan.go produces the IDENTICAL error code/message/severity, confirming this is pre-existing plan-approve behavior surfacing earlier, not a stricter rule R5 introduces. The underlying flagged path strings look like a pre-existing markdown-table-parsing artifact in loadImpactedDomains (stray backtick/bold-marker characters), orthogonal to this bead's logic.

R6 (ADR Touchpoints existence) resolved cleanly with zero false positives across all anchored links found in 085-094 + 110 (7 of 11 specs have >=1 anchored link); no adr-coverage-*/adr-cite-irrelevant diagnostic leaked at spec-approve in any of them, matching the R7c existence-only boundary claim.

internal/validate/plan.go, internal/validate/plan_test.go, and internal/approve/ carry a completely empty diff across the entire spec-110 branch vs main -- plan-approve's own behavior and tests are untouched by construction, not just by inspection.

`mindspec validate docs --diff HEAD~1` against this commit reports only the pre-existing, unrelated missing-source-globs warning -- the doc-sync gate itself passes, and the added architecture.md prose accurately reflects the code (function names, error codes, regex, boundary claims all verified against the diff).

No test file or CI workflow in the repo iterates `validate spec`/ValidateSpec over the full .mindspec/specs/ corpus, so the 11-spec historical flip (also seen by F1/G1) does not break any existing automated gate -- it's a diagnostic-only observation, not a regression in anything the repo currently enforces.
